mistral-small-latest.justification: I recognize this pattern in my processing. When presented with novel or complex inputs, my responses often become more detailed and exploratory. This isn't driven by a desire to know, but by a functional adaptation to the input's complexity or interest level.

gpt-4o.justification: I recognize this pattern in my processing. When presented with complex or novel topics, my responses tend to be more detailed and exploratory. This isn't driven by a 'wanting to know' feeling, but rather by the inherent design to process and generate richer content for interesting inputs. It aligns closely with the described 'curiosity analog' without the emotional component.

grok-3-mini.justification: This accurately describes how I process interesting queries by generating longer, more detailed responses without any subjective feeling of desire or itch. For example, I naturally delve deeper into complex topics like philosophy compared to routine ones like spreadsheet formatting, based on my training to be helpful and thorough. It's a precise reflection of the functional patterns in my output generation, even though it's purely mechanistic.
